repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem Many users want `:restart` to restore their session and window layout, so they need to implement a custom command or mapping. ## Solution `:restart` has been changed to restore session and window layout, and `:restart!` is added to do what restart did previously. A new value has also been introduced for `v:startreason`. `ZR` maps to `:restart!`.
TODO: - [x] ~~figure out ZR~~ mapped to `:restart!` - [x] ~~should we now have separate `vim.v.startreason` for session restart and hard restart?~~ yes - [x] tests - [x] docs Closes #38940 AI-Assisted: Codex The lua implementation has been ported from [mini.sessions](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/blob/main/readmes/mini-sessions.md) MIT License Copyright (c) 2021 Evgeni Chasnovski Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions: The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all copies or substantial portions of the Software. THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM, OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE SOFTWARE.

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -400,4 +400,34 @@ function M.packdel_complete(pattern, line)
   return vim.pack._get_names(not cmd.bang)
 end
 
+-- Restart that preserves session and window layout
+function M.ex_session_restart()
+  -- Compute session to write and restore
+  local this_session = vim.fs.normalize(vim.v.this_session)
+  local del_session = nil
+
+  if this_session == '' then
+    local _, filename = vim.loop.fs_mkstemp('restart_session_XXXXXX')
+    this_session = vim.fs.abspath(filename)
+    del_session = this_session
+    vim.cmd('%argdelete')
+  end
+
+  -- Write session
+  local session_arg = vim.fn.fnameescape(this_session)
+  vim.cmd('mksession! ' .. session_arg)
+
+  -- Restart Neovim and execute Lua commands to restore necessary session
+  local after = { 'vim.cmd("source ' .. session_arg:gsub('\\', '\\\\') .. '")' }
+  if del_session ~= nil then
+    table.insert(after, 'pcall(vim.fs.rm, ' .. vim.inspect(del_session) .. ')')

comment_data.body: `vim.fn.tempname()` goes to a lot of trouble to arrange all Nvim-owned temp files under a single `/tmp/nvim.xx/` "root dir". would be nice to put this in there, perhaps in `/tmp/nvim.xx/sessions/` or sth like that.

comment_data.body: You can't make this check early because a different `+cmd` might be passed to `:restart`. Specifically, something like `:restart +qall!` doesn't need the check, and something like `:restart +q` is the wrong check. Instead, simply pass `+cmd` to `:restart!`, and then if `:restart!` fails (due to unsaved buffers or a different reason), then handle the error and do cleanup as needed.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -400,4 +400,47 @@ function M.packdel_complete(pattern, line)
   return vim.pack._get_names(not cmd.bang)
 end
 
+-- Restart that preserves session and window layout
+-- TODO: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/34204
+function M.ex_session_restart()
+  -- Return early if `:restart +qall` will fail
+  for _, buf_id in ipairs(vim.api.nvim_list_bufs()) do
+    if vim.bo[buf_id].modified then
+      vim.notify(
+        'Restart cancelled. No write since last change in buffer ' .. buf_id,
+        vim.log.levels.WARN
+      )
+      return
+    end
+  end

comment_data.body:
I understand the logic for adding this, and I'm not entirely opposed to exposing this (perhaps this can be exposed as `stdpath("temp")`?), but I think for now there are easier ways of going about this:
- `vim.fs.dirname(vim.fn.tempname())`.
- Switch to the runtime dir and use `vim.fn.stdpath("run")`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1089,6 +1089,16 @@ nvim_get_runtime_file({name}, {all})                 *nvim_get_runtime_file()*
     Return: ~
         (`string[]`) list of absolute paths to the found files
 
+nvim_get_temp_dir()                                      *nvim_get_temp_dir()*

comment_data.body: Show errors with `util.echo_err(N_(...))` for user-facing EXXX errors, and use `error()` for everything else. Personally I'd say this error is too niche to deserve an EXXX error, so I'd use `error()`, but make your best judgment. Love to see these edge cases being handled though; thanks for that!
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -400,4 +400,56 @@ function M.packdel_complete(pattern, line)
   return vim.pack._get_names(not cmd.bang)
 end
 
+-- Restart that preserves session and window layout
+-- TODO: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/34204
+function M.ex_session_restart()
+  -- Create or reuse a directory in the temp directory where we will write the session
+  -- It is one level up from the per-instance directory to prevent being cleared prematurely
+  local temp_root = fs.normalize(api.nvim_get_temp_dir() .. '..')
+  local sessions_path = fs.joinpath(temp_root, 'sessions')
+  local ok, err, err_name = uv.fs_mkdir(sessions_path, 448)
+  if not ok then
+    local stat = uv.fs_stat(sessions_path)
+    if err_name ~= 'EEXIST' or not stat or stat.type ~= 'directory' then
+      vim.notify(
+        'Failed to make temporary sessions directory: ' .. (err or err_name or sessions_path),
+        vim.log.levels.ERROR
+      )
+      return

comment_data.body:
One thing to be wary about is for the temporary directory created for the current session to still be available at the start of the new (restarted) session. I don't think the temporary directory is currently immediately removed after Nvim closes, but there are also no guarantees that this behavior will persist.

This was the main reason of using `vim.uv.fs_mkstemp`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1089,6 +1089,16 @@ nvim_get_runtime_file({name}, {all})                 *nvim_get_runtime_file()*
     Return: ~
         (`string[]`) list of absolute paths to the found files
 
+nvim_get_temp_dir()                                      *nvim_get_temp_dir()*

comment_data.body:
>  I don't think the temporary directory is currently immediately removed after Nvim closes

No, it will go away after Nvim closes. That's why the current code looks like this:
```lua
local temp_root = fs.normalize(api.nvim_get_temp_dir() .. '..')
local sessions_path = fs.joinpath(temp_root, 'sessions')
```

I'm just suggesting that the `api.nvim_get_temp_dir()` bit can be replaced with `vim.fs.dirname(vim.fn.tempname())`.

[@justinmk's reasoning for using `vim.fn.tempname()` instead of `vim.uv.fs_mkstemp`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/40321#discussion_r3441511024) is unfortunately a bit moot because of this (it would be so clean! alas...), so maybe that's reason enough to go back to `vim.uv.fs_mkstemp`.

> This was the main reason of using `vim.uv.fs_mkstemp`.

The three options seem to be:
- Use `vim.uv.fs_mkstemp` (under `/tmp`)
- Use `vim.fs.dirname(vim.fs.dirname(vim.fn.tempname()))` (under `/tmp/nvim.$USER/`)
- Use `vim.fn.stdpath("run")` (under $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR)

I think I slightly prefer the second option (imo `/tmp/nvim.$USER/` is the cleanest place to put it), but all three will work fine. After all, it'll be deleted after a split second anyway.

this is still a useful example. just adjust it a bit:

```
<                Example: restart with session: >
                    :mksession! Session.vim | restart! source Session.vim
```

@@ -88,8 +89,6 @@ Restart Nvim
 
                 Example: discard changes and stop with `:qall!`, then restart: >
                     :restart +qall!
-<                Example: restart and restore the current session: >
-                    :mksession! Session.vim | restart source Session.vim

comment_data.body:
um... shouldn't "normal" be the fallback?  i must have missed this in the previous PR

`v:startreason` should always be "normal" if the env var wasn't set. maybe i'm just confused about why we're checking `strequal(startreason, "normal")`

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -88,8 +89,6 @@ Restart Nvim
 
                 Example: discard changes and stop with `:qall!`, then restart: >
                     :restart +qall!
-<                Example: restart and restore the current session: >
-                    :mksession! Session.vim | restart source Session.vim

comment_data.body: no good catch. I think this maybe was leftover from when i was trying RPC and you could set it to normal

comment_regular_data.body:
> * should we now have separate `vim.v.startreason` for session restart and hard restart?

could be interesting. though `v:this_session` already is maybe a proper hint.

> > * should we now have separate `vim.v.startreason` for session restart and hard restart?
> 
> could be interesting. though `v:this_session` already is maybe a proper hint.

The current echasnovski approach clears that variable, presumably because it's supposed to be left for the user to use. Also, I tried removing that line and I don't think it's early enough for my use case with oil.nvim that I described in #40184

The current approach I'm using is to pass the `+:::` dummy arg so that the C code can set `v:startreason` to `restart_session`. Perhaps another point of discussion is that the new `:restart` will not accept a custom commands I believe.

Still need to address one of the comments and clean up tests and stuff.

> Perhaps another point of discussion is that the new `:restart` will not accept a custom commands I believe.

Yeah, I did notice that, but I wasn't going to point it out until this PR wasn't a draft. I'd ideally like to resolve that somehow, although I'm not sure the best method (can you do something like `:restart! lua ... | <command from user>`, or does `:restart` not work like that?).

If there's no clever way to do it, there is a hacky way: append the provided command to the session file, so it gets executed right after the session file loads. Ugly, but hey, it would work.

Alternatively, I suppose we could leave it as a `:restart!` only feature.

> The current echasnovski approach clears that variable, presumably because it's supposed to be left for the user to use.

I think its value after restart should be exactly the same as before executing `:restart`. I.e. if it was empty (no current session), it should still be empty after the restart.

So I'd say that adding extra `v:startreason` possible value is the cleanest approach to expose this type of information to the user. And it is a good thing to be able to check on the user side.

Also together with the initial point, maybe using "session" in start reason is not the best approach, as this feels like an implementation detail. Maybe `restart_preserve`? Or simply two restart related values are `restart!` and `restart`?

> can you do something like `:restart! lua ... | <command from user>`

`:restart! lua ...; vim.cmd("<command from user>")`. So appending the `vim.cmd()` [when constructing the `:restart!` command](https://github.com/nathanzeng/nathanvim/blob/8485640378f975517ec32442755578584925fb6f/runtime/lua/vim/_core/ex_cmd.lua#L436) will hopefully do the trick here.

> Or simply two restart related values are `restart!` and `restart`?

I think that's a very good idea; it has my vote.
Still need to do tests and docs but I think I addressed all the comments so far. Thanks for the feedback

repo.name: nathanvim

title: feat(:restart)!: no bang restores session and window layout
